metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1254
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In this report, Geerts et al study the dynamic distribution of tomosyn-1 in mouse hippocampal neurons. By applying immunoelectron microscopy, immunocytochemistry and live imaging, the authors show localization of tomosyn-1 to dense core vesicles in EM images and indirectly by co-labelling with the DCV marker chromogranin B (Fig 2, 4). Furthermore, time-lapse images and coresponding kymographs supports these findings by showing co-migration tomosyn with neuropeptide Y, a DCV marker (Fig. 5).. Experimental description: Neuronal cultures
Animals were housed, bred and experimentally used according to Institutional guidelines and
Dutch and U.S. governmental laws with prior approval from the institutional animal research
ethics committee ("Dierexperimentencommissie Vrije Universiteit/VU medisch centrum";
approval number FGA11-03), ensuring minimum discomfort of animals. All procedures were
approved by the C57BL/6 wild type mice were obtained from Harlan (Horst, The Netherlands).
Tom-1KO/KO and Syt-1KO/KO mice were described [22,34]. E18 stage embryos were
obtained by Caesarean section of pregnant females from timed matings. H

5/2/2018 Pim
- Overexpressed Tomosyn-1 (syntaxin-binding protein 5) is of mouse species: "An EYFP-tag was fused to the N-terminus of mouse tomosyn-m1 (Genbank accession number NP_001074813.2) ..."
